title: Bank Holding Companies and Change in Bank Control (Regulation Y)
abstract: Section 2208 of the Economic Growth and Regulatory Paperwork Reduction Act of 1996 amended the Bank Holding Company Act to eliminate the requirement that bank holding companies seek Board approval before engaging de novo in permissible nonbanking activities listed in Regulation Y if the holding company is well-capitalized and meets certain other criteria specified in the statute. Section 2208 also established an expedited procedure for well-capitalized bank holding companies that meet these crit
action: Interim rule with request for comments.
issuing_agency: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
